title: KING COUNTY DIRECTORS ASSOCIATION – 3 EMPLOYEES - Request for PUBLIC RECORDS (2022-2026)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: KING COUNTY DIRECTORS ASSOCIATION – 3 EMPLOYEES - Request for PUBLIC RECORDS (2022-2026)
communications.communication:
KING COUNTY DIRECTORS ASSOCIATION – 3 EMPLOYEES - Request for PUBLIC RECORDS (2022-2026)

Pursuant to Washington State Public Records Law RCW 42.56, I hereby request the following records: 

RECORDS REQUEST PART 1: THREE (3) EMPLOYEE PERSONNEL FILES

I request the complete non-exempt Personnel File, including all payroll files, timesheets, absence & tardiness, disciplinary actions, W-2's, travel and expense reimbursements, any and all additional appropriations on behalf of (for example conference attendance reimbursements, hotel and flight vouchers, etc.) resumes, letters of recommendation and all other nonexempt hiring-related documents, position descriptions, compensation records, information on benefit enrollment, attendance records, performance evaluations and information about all other employment-related actions (e.g., promotions, training, or any disciplinary action), and any other non-exempt job-related information available for the for the following current or former employee of the King County Directors Association: 

DAVID SCOTT MALHALKO
YOHAN LEE
KAREN KAY DESKIN

Please provide all responsive records available for the time period 2017 – Present. 

RECORDS REQUEST PART 2: .PST FILE 2017-PRESENT 

I request a complete electronic copy of the email server .PST file for the email account associated with the following three (3) email addresses for the time period 2017 – Present:

dmalhalko@kcda.org
ylee@kcda.org
kdeskin@kcda.org

RECORDS INSTALLMENTS

If production of these email records will be completed in multiple installments, please begin by producing responsive email records in REVERSE chronological order by date, producing the most recent messages and records first.

SEARCH FOR RESPONSIVE RECORDS

Your agency’s search for responsive email records should involve a good-faith search of all public employee personal devices reasonably believed to contain requested responsive records regardless the type of device or account from which the communication was generated or where the device or data is stored (including personal devices such as personal cell phones, laptops, tablets, and/or computers etc. used from home to conduct public business during the COVID-19 Pandemic). Nissen v. Pierce County, 183 Wa.2d 863 (2015).

Also, please provide a reasonably detailed, nonconclusory affidavit attesting to the nature and extent of any search of any privately owned public employee computer, device, or account.

FORMAT OF PRODUCTION OF RESPONSIVE RECORDS

Please provide all requested email records in their NATIVE electronic format. Please do not instead create new records by printing electronic files then scanning and printing the newly scanned prints.

Please provide all email records as stored in their NATIVE .pst (personal storage table) format as stored on agency email servers.

If these email records cannot be produced in this NATIVE .pst format, explain in exact technical and procedural detail under the Washington State Public Records Act (RCW 42.56) WHY these electronic records cannot be produced as the electronic records are NATIVELY stored.

EXEMPTION LOGS

Please ensure any agency redactions or exemptions claimed in the production of responsive records are accompanied by a complete and detailed exemption log noting the valid legal reason for each exemption at each redaction location in the record as well as the specific number of pages if any that your agency redacts or withholds in their entirety.

FEES

In the event that there are fees, please inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request in strict compliance with all provisions of the Washington State Public Records Act.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. Again, this request does not seek a list of any kind and is NOT FOR A COMMERICAL PURPOSE.

PRIOR DESTRUCTION OF RECORDS 

If any potentially relevant records have been destroyed, please provide copies of the destruction or transfer slips as well as any other documentation pertaining to, mentioning, or describing said transfer or destruction, including, but not limited to, a statement that your agency has no additional copies of the records in question. 

Please ensure any agency redactions or exemptions claimed in the production of responsive records are accompanied by a complete and detailed exemption log noting the valid legal reason for each exemption at each redaction location in the record as well as the specific number of pages if any that your agency redacts or withholds in their entirety.

This request is not commercial in nature and does not seek a list of any kind in any or in any form. 

This request is not for profit. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Thank you.

title: King County Directors Association -  Digital copies of all LEGAL INVOICES and/or PAYMENTS to Attorneys and/or Law Firms (2024-2026)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: King County Directors Association -  Digital copies of all LEGAL INVOICES and/or PAYMENTS to Attorneys and/or Law Firms (2024-2026)
communications.communication:
June 05, 2026

Public Records request for Digital copies of all LEGAL INVOICES and/or PAYMENTS to Attorneys and/or Law Firms (2024-2026)

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act [RCW 42.56] I hereby request digital copies the following public records:

1. Digital copies of any/all invoices or bills for any/all legal services or legal representation provided to your AGENCY, your AGENCY BOARD OF DIRECTORS, and/or AGENCY employees or representatives.

2. Digital copies of any/all payments made by your AGENCY for any/all legal services provided to the AGENCY or AGENCY employees or AGENCY representatives.

3. Digital copies of any/all emails, texts, and/or communications of any kind regarding legal invoices and/or payment for legal services of any kind made by your AGENCY.

DATE RANGE

The date range for this request is January 1, 2024 to June 05, 2026.

RECORDS INSTALLMENTS - ORDER OF PRODUCTION – REASONABLE ESTIMATE OF TIME

If production of responsive records will be completed in multiple installments, please begin by producing records in REVERSE CHRONOLOGICAL FORMAT BY DATE. The most recent public records should be produced first and the oldest records produced last by date.

Please provide a “reasonable” estimate of time your agency intends to produce a first installment of records as per WAC 44-14-04003 (7) and RCW 42.56.520(1)(c). This time estimate should be an actual date such as May 31, 2024 rather than an undefined block of time in the future such as “within the next six months.” (Hikel v. City of Lynnwood, Dec. 27, 2016)

DEFINITION OF RECORDS AND WRITINGS

RECORDS AND WRITINGS as defined by RCW 42.56.010(4): any handwriting, typewriting, printing, photostating, photographing, and every other means of recording any form of communication or representation including, but not limited to, letters, words, pictures, sounds, or symbols, or combination thereof, and all papers, maps, magnetic or paper tapes, photographic films and prints, motion picture, film and video recordings, magnetic or punched cards, discs, drums, diskettes, sound recordings, and other documents including existing data compilations from which information may be obtained or translated.

PRODUCTION PROCESSING AND DELIVERY OF ELECTRONIC RECORDS

Please provide all records in their native electronic format. Please do not instead create new records by printing electronic files then scanning and printing the newly scanned prints.

Washington State Law is clear that the use of 3rd party “portals” (Such as GOVQA) for a public records request is optional for the requestor. Respectfully I decline. Please provide all records electronically via direct email attachment to this email address.

Please do not register this requestor for, nor direct this requestor to the use of any 3rd party commercial portal including but not limited to FOIAOnline, GovQA, NextRequest, FOIAExpress, JustFOIA FOIADirect, WebForm, or any other online service other than a direct NO PASSWORD REQUIRED, NO REGISTRATION REQUIRED, internet cloud-based download link, or electronic email.

Do not send any portion of this public records request to any 3rd party for-profit corporation such as GOVQA, a Grannicus Corporation or NEXT-REQUEST.

Do not register this request with any for profit portal or 3rd party organization such as GOVQA, a Grannicus Corporation, or NEXT-REQUEST.

PUBLIC RECORDS REQUESTS SUBMITTED VIA EMAIL

RCW 42.56.080 - Identifiable records—Facilities for copying—Availability of public records.

"...Agencies shall honor requests received in person during an agency's normal office hours, or by mail or email, for identifiable public records unless exempted by provisions of this chapter. No official format is required for making a records request; however, agencies may recommend that requestors submit requests using an agency provided form or web page."

This public records request is being made via email. This public records request will be honored and fulfilled by your agency via email as requested or relief will be sought per statute.

NATIVE ELECTRONIC FORMAT

Please provide all identified public records in their NATIVE electronic format, either .pst file or .pdf/ .pdf PORTFOLIO file. Please provide all electronic records in the fewest number of individual files as per native format or use free .zip or digital compression in each installment for the fewest number of total records and the most economical calculation of any applicable public records fees.

FEES

In the event that there are fees, please inform us of the total charges in advance of fulfilling the request in strict compliance with all provisions of the Washington State Public Records Act.

EXEMPTION LOGS

Please ensure all redactions or exemptions claimed by your agency in the production of responsive records are accompanied by a complete and detailed exemption log noting the valid legal reason for each exemption at each redaction location in the record produced, as well as the specific number of pages if any that your agency redacts or withholds in their entirety. Each redaction should be noted by footnote or by a clear reference to the specific justification for that redaction, and only the minimal exempt portion of any record may be withheld.

COMMERCIAL PURPOSE DECLARATION

The Washington State Public Records Act prohibits the disclosure of “lists of individuals” for a commercial purpose (RCW 42.56.070(8)). I declare this public records request is not being made for any commercial purpose whatsoever. Also, this public records request is not being made for a "list of individuals." This declaration satisfies all requirements of RCW 42.56 regarding prohibitions on lists and commercial purposes. No additional commercial purpose declaration will be completed for this public records request.

PRESERVATION OF PUBLIC RECORDS *** ANTICIPATE LITIGATION ***

WAC 44-14-03005 RETENTION OF PUBLIC RECORDS

The lawful destruction of public records is governed by retention schedules. The unlawful destruction of public records can be a crime. RCW 40.16.010 and 40.16.020. An agency is prohibited from destroying a public record, even if it is about to be lawfully destroyed under a retention schedule, if a public records request has been made for that record. RCW 42.56.100. Additional retention requirements might apply if the records may be relevant to actual or anticipated litigation. The agency is required to retain the record until the record request has been resolved.

Note: An agency can be found to violate the Public Records Act and be subject to the attorneys' fees and penalty provision if it prematurely destroys a requested record after a request is made. See Yacobellis v. City of Bellingham, 55 Wn. App. 706, 780 P.2d 272 (1989). However, it is not a violation of the Public Records Act if a record is destroyed prior to an agency's receipt of a public records request for that record. Bldg. Indus. Ass'n of Wash. v. McCarthy, 152 Wn. App. 720, 218 P.3d 196 (2009); West v. Dep't of
Nat. Res., 163 Wn. App. 238, 258 P.3d 78 (2011). The Public Records Act (chapter 42.56 RCW) and the records retention statutes (chapter 40.14 RCW) are two different laws.

COMMUNICATION VIA EMAIL

Please send all requests for clarification, status updates, and production of responsive electronic documents via direct email reply.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. We look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Good Luck.

title: Kennesaw Flock

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Kennesaw Flock
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

This request concerns Flock Safety cameras (including automated license plate reader / ALPR devices) within the City of Kennesaw, including records held by the Kennesaw Police Department, for the period from the inception of the City’s relationship with Flock (or the earliest date responsive records exist) through the present. All statutory references below are to the Georgia Open Records Act, O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70 et seq.

Definitions and Instructions (please apply throughout):

a) “Record” is used here as defined in O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70(b)(2), which defines “public record” to mean all documents, papers, letters, maps, books, tapes, photographs, computer based or generated information, data, data fields, or similar material prepared and maintained or received by an agency or by a private person or entity in the performance of a service or function for or on behalf of an agency. Consistent with that definition, this request covers responsive records in any format, including without limitation emails, text/SMS messages, messaging-app communications (e.g., Microsoft Teams, Slack), voicemails, calendar entries, drafts, metadata, attachments, and embedded files. Because the statutory definition turns on a record’s function rather than the device or account on which it resides, this request also covers responsive records concerning City business held on personal accounts or devices.

b) “Flock” means Flock Safety, Flock Group Inc., and any parent, subsidiary, affiliate, or authorized reseller; and “the cameras” / “the system” means any automated or fixed/mobile license plate reader (ALPR/LPR), camera, sensor, or associated software or network supplied, hosted, operated, or serviced by the foregoing, regardless of the trade or brand name used.

c) Scope of custodians: this request applies to responsive records held by any City of Kennesaw department or official, including but not limited to the Police Department, the Office of the Mayor and City Council, the City Manager, Finance/Procurement, Legal, and Information Technology.

d) Vendor-held records: pursuant to O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70(b)(2), records maintained by Flock or any vendor on the City’s behalf are public records subject to disclosure to the same extent as if held by the City. Please obtain and produce responsive vendor-held records accordingly.

e) Format: please produce records in their native electronic format with metadata intact; do not convert searchable electronic records into image-only files. Availability of any data on a public website does not satisfy a request for that data in its native electronic format.

Records Requested:

1) All contracts, agreements, memoranda of understanding, statements of work, purchase orders, amendments, and renewals with Flock.

2) All invoices, payment records, and budget documents identifying the funding source, including any grant applications or awards.

3) All records identifying who authorized the deployment — City Council agendas, minutes, votes, resolutions, and any internal memoranda or staff reports.

4) All communications (including emails, text messages, and messaging-app messages, with attachments) between City or Police officials and Flock, and among City personnel, regarding acquisition, deployment, renewal, or use of the system.

5) Records sufficient to show the number and physical location of every installed camera.

6) All policies, standard operating procedures, and access/usage policies governing the cameras and the collected data.

7) The data retention schedule and policy.

8) All data-sharing agreements and records identifying every outside entity (other agencies, state or federal partners, the national Flock network) with access to the data.

9) Existing audit logs and search/query logs reflecting use of the system, in the format the system already maintains or can export. I am not asking the City to create a new compilation; I request the standard logs/exports as they exist.

10) Records identifying the sources of any “hot lists” used by the system.

11) Any privacy impact assessment, civil-liberties review, or community-notification records.

12) All training materials provided to staff regarding the system.

Procedural Requests:

– If any category is voluminous and cannot be produced within three business days, please provide the statutorily required description, timeline, and cost estimate rather than a denial.

– For any record or portion withheld or redacted, please notify me of the specific legal authority for withholding by Code section, subsection, and paragraph, as required by O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(d) (the exemptions being enumerated in O.C.G.A. § 50-18-72), and produce all reasonably segregable non-exempt material.

– If you locate no responsive records for any numbered item, please state which custodians and systems were searched and the search terms used.

– Please use the most economical means reasonably calculated to identify and produce responsive records, as required by O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(c)(1).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

QS Intelligence Group

title: KING COUNTY DIRECTORS' ASSOCIATION – Board of Directors Meeting(s) Records (2022)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: KING COUNTY DIRECTORS' ASSOCIATION – Board of Directors Meeting(s) Records (2022)
communications.communication:
KING COUNTY DIRECTORS' ASSOCIATION – Board of Directors Meeting(s) Records (2022)

June 04, 2026

via email to: Kristen Deskin, Human Resources Manager and Public Records Officer <kdeskin@kcda.org>

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act [RCW 42.56], I hereby request the following records:

1. Produce copies of all "Records and Writings" of all minutes, complete meeting packets, resolutions, audio or video recordings, executive session logs, and presentations, inclusive, for every meeting of the KCDA Board of Directors for the year 2022, including all records provided to the Board by Executive Directors Bart Powelson or David Mahalko regarding litigation updates, vendor awards, or funding-out contract terminations.

DEFINITION OF RECORDS AND WRITINGS

"RECORDS AND WRITINGS" as defined by RCW 42.56.010(4): any handwriting, typewriting, printing, photostating, photographing, and every other means of recording any form of communication or representation including, but not limited to, letters, words, pictures, sounds, or symbols, or combination thereof, and all papers, maps, magnetic or paper tapes, photographic films and prints, motion picture, film and video recordings, magnetic or punched cards, discs, drums, diskettes, sound recordings, and other documents including existing data compilations from which information may be obtained or translated.

PRODUCTION PROCESSING AND DELIVERY OF ELECTRONIC RECORDS

Please provide all records in their native electronic format. Please do not instead create new records by printing electronic files then scanning and printing the newly scanned prints.

To preserve metadata and ease review, please produce as follows:

Emails: PST (Microsoft Outlook) or MBOX containers.

Chats/Texts/IM: Native exports where supported; otherwise, CSV/JSON with fields for sender, recipients, timestamps (UTC), message/thread IDs, and attachments (export attachments in original formats).

Calendars: ICS where possible; or PDF plus any attachments.

Documents/attachments: Original/native formats (DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, PDF, etc.).

Logs/Reports: CSV/JSON preferred.

Delivery: Direct electronic transmission via email attachments or open (no login/registration/password) download link hosted by your agency (e.g., SharePoint/OneDrive public link). Please do not use third-party portals that require creating an account or logging in. A single or series of .zip files is acceptable.

If any records cannot be produced in the above formats, please produce them in their native format with associated metadata intact.

METADATA REQUESTED

Where reasonably available, please include customary metadata fields such as: From, To, CC, BCC, Subject, Sent/Received Date-Time (with timezone/UTC), Message-ID, Conversation/Thread-ID, file names, file sizes, hash values (if held), and folder/location paths.

SEARCH DETAILS AND RECORDS PRESERVATION

Please identify the systems searched e.g. (Exchange/Outlook/Office 365, Teams, mobile devices, MDM archives, JustFOIA/CivicPlus admin, helpdesk, file shares) and the custodians.

Please describe the search methods/terms used.

If any potentially responsive sources were not searched or are no longer available, please explain why and what preservation steps were taken.

USE OF THIRD-PARTY PORTALS NOT MANDATORY

Please note that under Washington’s Public Records Act, use of a third-party “portal” is not mandatory for requestors. Agencies may not condition access to records on the creation of an account, the use of a login, or the acceptance of third-party vendor terms.

Third-party portals (such as GovQA, NextRequest, JustFOIA, or similar systems) often:

Require account creation or login credentials;

Restrict downloads, viewing times, or re-access;

Remove or disable links after a limited period;

Place records behind commercial platforms operated by for-profit corporations.

These restrictions unlawfully burden and condition access to public records. The Public Records Act forbids agencies from imposing access barriers beyond those authorized by statute.

RCW 42.56.080(2): An agency “shall not deny a request for identifiable public records solely on the basis that the request is overbroad.” Agencies must provide the “fullest assistance” and “most timely possible action” to requestors.

RCW 42.56.550(1): Courts are directed to liberally construe the PRA to ensure access and strictly construe exemptions against withholding.

Fisher Broadcasting-Seattle TV LLC v. City of Seattle, 180 Wn.2d 515, 528 (2014): Agencies must produce records in a “reasonably usable format when feasible.”

Mechling v. City of Monroe, 152 Wn. App. 830, 841 (2009): PRA is to be liberally construed in favor of disclosure and cannot be restricted by agency practices.

Because of this, I respectfully decline use of any third-party portal. Please provide all responsive records via:

Direct electronic email attachment to this requestor’s address, or a direct, open (no password, no registration required) cloud-based download link hosted by your agency.

Do not register this requestor with, nor direct this requestor to, any third-party commercial portal, including but not limited to FOIAOnline, GovQA (a Granicus Corporation), NextRequest, FOIAExpress, JustFOIA, FOIADirect, or any similar online service.

Do not transmit any portion of this request, or responsive records, to any for-profit vendor or portal. Doing so would unlawfully condition access to public records and contravene both RCW 42.56.080 and RCW 42.56.550.

PUBLIC RECORDS REQUESTS SUBMITTED VIA EMAIL

This public records request is being submitted via email, consistent with Washington’s Public Records Act.

RCW 42.56.080(2) provides in relevant part: “Agencies shall honor requests received in person during an agency’s normal office hours, or by mail or email, for identifiable public records unless exempted by provisions of this chapter. No official format is required for making a records request; however, agencies may recommend that requestors submit requests using an agency-provided form or web page.”

Accordingly:

A request submitted by email is a valid, enforceable method of making a PRA request.

Agencies may recommend, but cannot require, the use of an online portal or official form.

Any refusal to honor this request as submitted would directly conflict with RCW 42.56.080 and the agency’s duty under RCW 42.56.100 to provide the “fullest assistance to requestors” and adopt reasonable rules that do not “unreasonably hinder or delay” access.

Therefore, this request is to be honored and fulfilled by your agency via email, as submitted. Should the agency fail to process this request in compliance with statute, relief will be sought pursuant to RCW 42.56.550 in the form of judicial review, penalties, and attorney’s fees.

INSTALLMENTS AND FEE ESTIMATES

Please provide an initial acknowledgment within five business days (RCW 42.56.520) with a reasonable time estimate for each installment, and

A cost estimate if fees are anticipated (please itemize and explain).

NISSEN AFFIDAVIT AND ALL DEVICE SEARCH REQUIRED

Pursuant to Nissen v. Pierce County, 183 Wn.2d 863 (2015), and its progeny, this request specifically includes all responsive public records contained on personal devices or personal accounts (including personal cell phones, text/SMS/MMS, personal email accounts, cloud storage, instant messaging apps, and social media accounts) of any agency employee, official, or contractor where public business may have been conducted.

To comply with this requirement, please:

Conduct a comprehensive all-device search, including both agency-issued and personally owned devices/accounts used for public business.

Require each potentially responsive custodian to execute a Nissen affidavit or sworn certification affirming:

1. They conducted a diligent search of all personal and agency devices/accounts reasonably likely to contain responsive records;

2. The methods used to conduct the search (e.g., search terms, accounts checked, device types); and

3. Whether any responsive records were located, preserved, and produced.

Produce all responsive records located in such searches along with the executed affidavits.

Failure to obtain and provide sworn Nissen affidavits or equivalent certifications from custodians may constitute an incomplete search in violation of RCW 42.56.520 (requirement for a reasonable and adequate search) and may expose the agency to relief under RCW 42.56.550.

EXEMPTION LOGS 

Please ensure that all redactions or exemptions claimed by your agency in the production of responsive records are accompanied by a complete and detailed exemption log.

Exemptions logs at minimum must contain the following:

1. Identify the valid legal reason (statutory citation) for each exemption;

2. Specify the location of each redaction within the record produced (via footnote, coded reference, or margin notation);

3. State the number of pages or records withheld in their entirety, if any; and

4. Demonstrate that only the minimal exempt portion of any record has been withheld, as required by law.

The Washington Public Records Act requires agencies to justify each withholding or redaction:

RCW 42.56.210(3): “Agency responses refusing, in whole or in part, inspection of any public record shall include a statement of the specific exemption authorizing the withholding of the record (or part) and a brief explanation of how the exemption applies to the record withheld.”

RCW 42.56.210(1): Exemptions must be narrowly construed.

Washington courts have repeatedly enforced this duty:

Rental Housing Ass’n of Puget Sound v. City of Des Moines, 165 Wn.2d 525, 538 (2009): Agencies must provide specific explanations for each exemption; conclusory or vague justifications are insufficient.

Sanders v. State, 169 Wn.2d 827, 845 (2010): “The burden of proof is on the agency to establish that refusal to permit public inspection and copying is in accordance with a statute.”

Resident Action Council v. Seattle Housing Auth., 177 Wn.2d 417, 433 (2013): Agencies must disclose the non-exempt portions of records and cannot withhold more than necessary.

Therefore, I request that your agency provide an itemized exemption log with each installment of records produced, in compliance with RCW 42.56.210(3) and the above case law, ensuring transparency and accountability in the redaction process.

COMMERCIAL PURPOSE DECLARATION

This public records request is not being made for any commercial purpose. The Washington State Public Records Act (PRA) prohibits agencies from disclosing “lists of individuals” for commercial purposes.

RCW 42.56.070(8) states: “This chapter shall not be construed as giving authority to any agency to give, sell, or provide access to lists of individuals requested for commercial purposes….”

Accordingly:

This request does not seek a “list of individuals.”

This request is not made for any commercial purpose.

This declaration satisfies all requirements of RCW 42.56.070(8) regarding prohibitions on lists and commercial purposes.

Warning: The PRA does not authorize agencies to require requestors to complete additional “commercial purpose declarations” when the request does not seek a list of individuals. Such requirements improperly condition access to public records in violation of RCW 42.56.080(2) (requests must be honored by mail or email, no official format required) and RCW 42.56.100 (agencies must provide the fullest assistance to requestors and may not adopt rules that unreasonably hinder or delay access).

Washington courts have consistently rejected agency attempts to impose conditions not found in statute:

Mechling v. City of Monroe, 152 Wn. App. 830, 841 (2009): The PRA is a strongly worded mandate for broad disclosure; agencies cannot erect unauthorized barriers.

SEIU Local 925 v. University of Washington, 193 Wn.2d 860, 875 (2019): The PRA is to be liberally construed to promote access, and exemptions must be narrowly applied.

Therefore, no additional commercial purpose declaration will be completed for this request. Any attempt to impose such a requirement would be an unlawful restriction of rights under RCW 42.56.

PRESERVATION OF PUBLIC RECORDS *** ANTICIPATE LITIGATION *** WAC 44-14-03005 RETENTION OF PUBLIC RECORDS

WAC 44-14-03005 (Retention of Public Records) makes clear that public records are subject to retention schedules, and that unlawful destruction can constitute a crime.

RCW 40.16.010 and RCW 40.16.020 criminalize the unlawful mutilation, destruction, or concealment of public records.

RCW 42.56.100 expressly prohibits an agency from destroying any public record after a public records request has been made for that record, even if the record would otherwise be scheduled for lawful destruction.

In addition, agencies have heightened retention obligations when litigation is reasonably anticipated. Records potentially relevant to actual or anticipated litigation must be preserved until both the public records request and the litigation have been fully resolved.

Legal Consequences of Premature Destruction: In Yacobellis v. City of Bellingham, 55 Wn. App. 706, 780 P.2d 272 (1989), the Court of Appeals held that an agency violates the Public Records Act and is subject to attorneys’ fees and statutory penalties when it destroys responsive records after receiving a public records request.

By contrast, it is not a PRA violation if a record was destroyed prior to the agency’s receipt of a request, provided the destruction was lawful under retention schedules. See Bldg. Indus. Ass’n of Wash. v. McCarthy, 152 Wn. App. 720, 218 P.3d 196 (2009); West v. Dep’t of Natural Resources, 163 Wn. App. 238, 258 P.3d 78 (2011).

Washington courts have emphasized that the Public Records Act (RCW 42.56) and the state’s records retention laws (RCW 40.14) are separate but complementary. Agencies must comply with both.

Accordingly:

Your agency must preserve all potentially responsive records immediately upon receipt of this request.

No responsive records may be altered, deleted, or destroyed until this request has been fully resolved and any related litigation constraints have been lifted.

Any unlawful destruction of responsive records may expose the agency and its officials to criminal liability (RCW 40.16), civil penalties, and mandatory attorneys’ fees (RCW 42.56.550(4)).

RECORDS INSTALLMENTS - ORDER OF PRODUCTION

If production of responsive records will be completed in multiple installments, production in reverse chronological order is strongly preferred to the extent supported by native system exports, provided it does not delay the delivery of installments.

DIGITAL COMPRESSION AND FILE MINIMIZATION REQUESTED IN ACCORDANCE WITH RCW 42.56

Pursuant to RCW 42.56.080(2), please provide all responsive electronic records in the fewest number of individual files, preserving native formats wherever possible. Where appropriate, please use free digital compression (.zip or equivalent) to consolidate responsive records into the smallest number of installments for efficiency and economy.

This approach reduces agency burden, promotes prompt disclosure, and minimizes potential costs to the requestor, consistent with the Public Records Act’s directive that agencies provide records in the most “reasonably locatable form” and “fullest assistance to requestors.” See RCW 42.56.100 (agency duty to adopt reasonable rules for providing fullest assistance); RCW 42.56.120(2)(b) (fees must reflect actual costs, not used to discourage access).

Washington courts have consistently held that agencies must facilitate access in a practical, non-burdensome manner. See Fisher Broadcasting-Seattle TV LLC v. City of Seattle, 180 Wn.2d 515, 528 (2014) (agencies must provide records in a usable format when reasonably feasible); Mechling v. City of Monroe, 152 Wn. App. 830, 841 (2009) (PRA is to be liberally construed in favor of disclosure). Producing consolidated, digitally compressed files satisfies these obligations by reducing unnecessary fragmentation and excess per-page charges.

Accordingly, I request that each installment be produced as a single compressed .zip file (or as few as technically necessary), containing all responsive records in native format, to ensure both compliance with RCW 42.56 and an economical calculation of any applicable public records fees.

COMMUNICATION VIA EMAIL

Please send all requests for clarification, status updates, and production of responsive electronic documents via direct email reply.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. We look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Good Luck.

communications.communication:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records related to the agency's Flock Safety automated license plate reader (ALPR) system:

Organization Audit in Flock, from April 1, 2026 to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency. Please include, for each search, the date and time, the searching user or unit, and the reason or justification entered in the audit trail.

Network Audit in Flock, for the same period. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the agency's Flock network by any agency in the Flock System. Please include, for each search, the date and time, the searching agency, and the reason or justification entered.

A current list of network share settings, including: (a) Flock networks shared with this agency ("shared with me"); (b) networks this agency is sharing with others ("networks I'm sharing"); and (c) any sharing with or access by other state, out-of-state, or federal agencies — including ICE, CBP, or DHS — and the date any such setting was enabled or disabled.

The agency's General Order, directive, or written policy governing ALPR/Flock use, including operator access, the search-justification requirement, data-retention period, audit procedures, and data sharing.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ryan Becker

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records related to the agency's Flock Safety automated license plate reader (ALPR) system:

Organization Audit in Flock, from April 1, 2026 to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency. Please include, for each search, the date and time, the searching user or unit, and the reason or justification entered in the audit trail.

Network Audit in Flock, for the same period. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the agency's Flock network by any agency in the Flock System. Please include, for each search, the date and time, the searching agency, and the reason or justification entered.

A current list of network share settings, including: (a) Flock networks shared with this agency ("shared with me"); (b) networks this agency is sharing with others ("networks I'm sharing"); and (c) any sharing with or access by other state, out-of-state, or federal agencies — including ICE, CBP, or DHS — and the date any such setting was enabled or disabled.

The agency's General Order, directive, or written policy governing ALPR/Flock use, including operator access, the search-justification requirement, data-retention period, audit procedures, and data sharing.

Please provide the audit and share-setting records in their original electronic, machine-readable format (CSV or spreadsheet). I request that personally identifying information of non-employee subjects be redacted rather than the records withheld. Please do not redirect me to an online transparency portal in lieu of producing these records.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ryan Becker

title: George W. Bush Presidential Library:  Divestiture of Columbian Chemicals Company by Phelps Dodge Corporation (2005–2007)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: George W. Bush Presidential Library:  Divestiture of Columbian Chemicals Company by Phelps Dodge Corporation (2005–2007)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act (5 U.S.C. § 552) and, to the extent applicable, the Presidential Records Act (44 U.S.C. §§ 2201–2209), I hereby request access to and copies of all records — including policy memoranda, email correspondence, inter-agency briefing papers, and economic reports — created or received by the Executive Office of the President (EOP) between January 1, 2005, and January 1, 2008, regarding the sale and divestiture of Columbian Chemicals Company by Phelps Dodge Corporation.

Scope of Request:

I am specifically seeking records from the National Economic Council (NEC), the National Security Council (NSC), and the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) that discuss:

The March 2006 transaction wherein Phelps Dodge sold its wholly owned specialty chemicals subsidiary, Columbian Chemicals Company, to a joint venture owned by One Equity Partners (JPMorgan Chase) and DC Chemical Co. Ltd. (South Korea).

Any inter-agency or White House communications analyzing the vulnerability of U.S. rubber, defense, and automotive supply chains resulting from the transfer of major carbon black manufacturing assets to foreign or private equity control.

Any records detailing the CFIUS (Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States) review process or national security clearance protocols triggered by South Korean-based DC Chemical Co. Ltd.'s acquisition of an ownership stake in domestic U.S. chemical infrastructure — specifically including the Marietta, Georgia headquarters and the Calumet, Louisiana processing facility (St. Mary Parish).

Correspondence or meeting minutes involving representatives of JPMorgan Chase / One Equity Partners and White House economic advisors regarding the financialization and restructuring of the domestic specialty chemicals sector during this period.

Statutory Acknowledgment regarding Section 721(c):

I recognize that Section 721(c) of the Defense Production Act (50 U.S.C. § 4565) provides a statutory exemption for information filed with CFIUS. However, this request specifically excludes the raw "notice" or "declaration" filed by the private corporate parties. Instead, it seeks the derivative executive branch records — such as policy memoranda, NSC/NEC briefing slides, trade impact studies, and inter-agency correspondence — that discuss the broader national security and macroeconomic implications of the carbon black manufacturing industry and the structural shift toward private equity ownership of critical infrastructure. I request that all reasonably segregable, non-exempt portions of these records be released pursuant to 5 U.S.C. § 552(b).

Search Keywords:
To facilitate an exhaustive search of the Electronic Records Archive (ERA), please utilize the following keywords:
"Columbian Chemicals", "Phelps Dodge", "One Equity Partners", "OEP", "JPMorgan Chase", "DC Chemical", "OCI Company", "Carbon Black", "CFIUS", "Specialty Chemicals Divestiture", "Marietta", "Calumet", "St. Mary Parish"

If a search results in the determination that certain parts of this request or certain portions must be redacted please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed.

I am studying the Kremlin's efforts to use social media to meddle in American politics and I believe that the ownership of Columbian Chemicals was an economic factor that drove an early attempts by the Kremlin to meddle in American culture on social media. This request is in the public interest. Thank you for everything the Bush library does and I look forward to working with you on the resolution of this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Flock Safety and ALPR records affecting Grayson
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Please route this request to the City Clerk or other designated Open Records Act officer for the City of Grayson.

This is a limited Georgia Open Records Act request for existing records concerning Flock Safety, Flock Group, automated license plate readers, ALPR systems, vehicle recognition cameras, or related camera-sharing systems affecting the City of Grayson, Georgia.

For the period January 1, 2023 through the date this request is processed, please provide:

1. Any contracts, purchase orders, invoices, quotes, proposals, statements of work, grants, memoranda of understanding, data-sharing agreements, intergovernmental agreements, letters of intent, renewal documents, or other agreement documents between the City of Grayson and Flock Safety, Flock Group, Gwinnett County, Gwinnett County Police Department, Gwinnett County Sheriff, or any other party concerning Flock Safety, ALPR cameras, vehicle recognition cameras, or camera-sharing systems.

2. Records sufficient to show whether any Flock Safety, ALPR, or vehicle recognition cameras have been installed, approved, proposed, funded, permitted, discussed, or located within or near the City of Grayson. This includes any existing list, map, agenda packet, staff report, permit record, site plan, right-of-way approval, pole-use approval, or similar record showing camera locations, proposed locations, owner/operator, funding source, or city involvement.

3. Any City Council meeting agendas, minutes, agenda packets, staff reports, presentations, ordinances, resolutions, public safety plans, or legal opinions from January 1, 2023 to the present that mention Flock Safety, Flock Group, ALPR, automated license plate readers, vehicle recognition cameras, camera sharing, surveillance cameras, or public safety cameras.

4. Any current City of Grayson policy, ordinance, resolution, agreement, or written procedure governing approval, permitting, funding, access, retention, oversight, or use of Flock Safety cameras, ALPR cameras, vehicle recognition cameras, or camera-sharing systems.

5. If the City maintains an easily searchable email system, please provide only emails from January 1, 2024 to the present between the City Administrator, City Clerk, Mayor, City Council members, or City Attorney and representatives of Flock Safety or Flock Group that contain any of the following terms: “Flock,” “ALPR,” “license plate reader,” “vehicle recognition,” or “camera sharing.” Please do not begin a broad email search if it will cause the estimated cost to exceed $25 without first providing an itemized estimate.

Please provide records in their original electronic format where available, including PDF, CSV, XLSX, native email export, or other machine-readable format.

If any portion of a record is withheld, please release all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions and cite the specific Georgia Code section, subsection, and paragraph relied upon for each withholding or redaction.

Please notify me before incurring costs over $25. If the agency estimates that the cost will exceed $25, please provide an itemized estimate and process items 1 through 4 first before performing any email search.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Don Page

University of Arizona ************************************************************************ Your record request #26-318 has been submitted successfully. Hello, This email acknowledges receipt of your Public Records Request directed to the University of Arizona. Please refer to your public records request number when making inquiries regarding your request. Please note, the university's rates for fulfilling public records requests are as follows: ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Non-commercial Requests Copying Fees: * * First 50 Pages: Free * Beyond 50 Pages: * Electronic Format: 20¢ per page (per side) * Black and White Paper Format: 25¢ per page (per side) * Oversize or Color Copies: Additional fees may apply if the reproduction cost exceeds 25¢ per page * Staff Time: $25.00 per hour, charged in quarter-hour increments, for document preparation ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Commercial Requests In addition to the copying fees mentioned above, commercial requests will incur extra fees, including: * * A portion of the cost to the University of Arizona for obtaining the original or copies of the records * A reasonable fee for the cost of equipment and personnel used in producing the record or reproduction * The value of the record or reproduction in the commercial market ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- If the Office of Public Records has enough information to move forward with the processing of your request, a preliminary estimate will be assessed utilizing the corresponding fee schedule(s) above. Once the estimate is crafted, the Office of Public Records will provide you with the corresponding estimate and will give you the opportunity to indicate if you would like to continue moving forward. Should you opt to accept the estimate and move forward, the Office of Public Records will continue to process the request, and once the records have been reviewed and finalized, a final invoice inclusive of the final costs will be provided to you for payment prior to disclosure. If any questions arise while you await the preliminary estimate, please don’t hesitate to reach out, thank you. The due date for your request may be later than usual because the office is closed on: * June 19: Juneteenth National Independence Day * July 4: Independence Day * September 7: Labor Day * November 11: Veterans Day * November 26: Thanksgiving * December 25: Christmas Day * January 1: New Year's Day * January 18: Martin Luther King, Jr. Day * February 15: Presidents' Day * May 31: Memorial Day ************************************************************************ Reply to this email or sign in to contact University of Arizona.
